Chapter 21  The Help Menu

Source: https://www.pcjs.org/documents/books/mspl13/basic/qblearn/

## Overview

Reference for the Help menu commands: accessing the keyword Index,
table of Contents, context-sensitive Topic help, and Help on Help.

## Help Menu Commands

| Command       | Shortcut     | Description                            |
|---------------|-------------|----------------------------------------|
| Index         | —           | Alphabetical list of all keywords      |
| Contents      | —           | Table of contents for on-line help     |
| Topic         | F1          | Help on keyword/symbol at cursor       |
| Help On Help  | SHIFT+F1    | How to use the help system             |

## 21.1  Index Command

Displays an alphabetical list of all QuickBASIC keywords. Organized
into lettered sections (A, B, C, etc.).

To find a keyword:
1. Choose Help > Index
2. Press the initial letter of the keyword
3. Select the keyword and press F1 for its help screen

## 21.2  Contents Command

Displays the table of contents for on-line help, organized by topic:

### Using QuickBASIC
- Shortcut Key Summary (Edit, View, Search, Run/Debug, Help Keys)
- Limits to QuickBASIC
- Version 4.5 Differences
- Survival Guide

### BASIC Programming Language
- Functional Keyword Lists
- Syntax Notation Conventions
- Fundamental Building Blocks
- Data Types
- Expressions and Operators
- Modules and Procedures
- Programs
- ASCII Character Codes
- Keyboard Scan Codes

Place cursor on a topic and press F1 for detailed information.

## 21.3  Topic Command

Displays help on the keyword or symbol nearest to the left of the cursor.

Shortcut: F1

Works on:
- BASIC keywords (PRINT, IF, FOR, etc.)
- User-defined variables and procedures
- TYPE members
- Error messages

## 21.4  Help On Help Command

Displays information about how to use the on-line help system.

Shortcut: SHIFT+F1

Covers:
- Navigating help screens
- Using hyperlinks
- Copying code from help
- Help keyboard shortcuts
